title: Explicit Rational Group Law on Hyperelliptic Jacobians of any Genus
abstract: It is well-known that abelian varieties are projective, and so that there exist explicit polynomial and rational functions which define both the variety and its group law. It is however difficult to find any explicit polynomial and rational functions describing these varieties or their group laws in dimensions greater than two. One exception can be found in Mumford's classic "Lectures on Theta", where he describes how to obtain an explicit model for hyperelliptic Jacobians as the union of several affine pieces described as the vanishing locus of explicit polynomial equations. In this article, we extend this work to give explicit equations for the group law on a dense open set. One can view these equations as generalizations of the usual chord-based group law on elliptic curves.
comment: The work has associated source code, a link to which is included in the references, and can be found at csclub.uwaterloo.ca/~dburbani/work/jacarith_dburbani_August2018.zip
